methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $370M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 13 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Quantum-Systems, a Series C defense and security company founded by Florian Seibel, has raised $370M+ to date and carries a current valuation of €3B. The valuation analysis triangulates two methods against a peer universe of 13 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, including 8 private firms and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $370M total raised proxy, yielding $629M. This captures typical step-up dynamics from Series C to subsequent funding rounds. Peer-median analysis across the 13-company defense & security cohort produces $1.2B, with a distribution spanning $300M (low) to $9.2B (high), where Saronic and Chaos Industries anchor the upper end at $9.2B and $4.5B respectively, and Series B peers like Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI cluster near $1.4–$1.5B.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $535M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $940M. This range reflects material dispersion within the peer set, driven largely by stage variance and company-specific risk profiles. Key uncertainties include technology maturity, customer concentration in defense contracting, and near-term funding trajectory. The current €3B valuation sits materially above the high end of this range and warrants careful scrutiny of non-public operational metrics and market positioning relative to peers.
